fixturePurpose: Documentation-only example for ACP-GAP-001; this file is not a production schema and does not bypass artifact validation.

producer.trustBoundary: Claude Code task output is producer input. Repository validation, reviewer disposition, and schema-backed artifacts remain authoritative.

source.entrypoint: Claude Code task in a dedicated repository worktree
source.handoffSurface: Task response, tool log summary, and continuation notes

rawSignals.value: Read target files, edited implementation, and ran a focused test suite.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: Tool log summary is not sufficient evidence until the main agent inspects the diff and validates commands.

rawSignals.value: Full build and PR review remain pending.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: Continuation notes should be routed to ae-handoff/v1 and hook-feedback/v1.

rawSignals.value: One broader validation command was not run in the producer session.
rawSignals.rawAssertionBoundary: Missing validation remains unresolved until a reviewer or CI supplies evidence.

commands.command: pnpm -s exec vitest run tests/example.test.ts --reporter dot
commands.evidenceUse: Focused behavior evidence; broader validation may still be missing.
Missing command should be surfaced as hook-feedback/v1 or PR-body note.

claimsMentioned.rawAssertion: The focused test suite covers the changed behavior.
claimsMentioned.controlPlaneBoundary: A focused test can support a claim but does not prove the full product state.

expectedArtifacts.purpose: Preserve files touched, completed steps, unresolved risks, and next decisions.
Tell the producer or main agent that broader validation remains missing.
Normalize changed-file scope after the main agent validates the diff.
Represent any supported or unresolved claims using existing claim status vocabulary.

knownGaps.summary: The fixture documents example raw signals without making Claude Code output a contract.
A future report-only normalizer can map this shape to handoff and feedback artifacts.
